h2h_summary: Germany and Paraguay have met 3 times in total, with a perfectly even all-time record of 1 win each and 1 draw. Germany has scored 4 goals across these head-to-head matches.
wc_history_home: Germany are four-time World Cup champions (1954, 1974, 1990, 2014) and the most successful European nation in the tournament's history, though they suffered back-to-back group-stage exits in 2018 and 2022.
wc_history_away: Paraguay have appeared at nine World Cups, reaching their best finish of the quarter-finals in 2010 before losing 1-0 to eventual champions Spain; this 2026 tournament marks their return after a 16-year absence.
group_context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
strengths_home: Exceptional creative duo Musiala and Wirtz capable of breaking down any defence
High-press intensity and gegenpressing system that wins back possession quickly
Clinical attack — 5 wins from last 5 competitive matches scoring 18 goals
strengths_away: Exceptional defensive discipline — conceded only 10 goals in qualifying, fewest in CONMEBOL
Dangerous transition game through Miguel Almiron's pace and directness
Strong set-piece organisation and compact midfield press
team_style_home: Germany under Nagelsmann use a high-energy 4-2-3-1 or 4-3-3 with aggressive gegenpressing, fast ball circulation, and fluid interchanges between Musiala and Wirtz to unlock defences through combinative play in tight spaces.
team_style_away: Paraguay under Gustavo Alfaro deploy a disciplined 4-4-2 or 4-2-3-1 built on defensive solidity and collective pressing intensity, relying on structured low blocks and rapid counter-attacks through Almiron's transition quality rather than sustained possession.
recent_news: Germany opened its World Cup play with a bang, throttling Curaçao 7-1 behind 2 goals from star Kai Havertz. Germany ended with a 2-1 win over Ivory Coast and a 2-1 loss to Ecuador. Paraguay settled for a goalless draw with Australia on matchday three to earn a spot in the knockout as one of the best third-placed teams.
context: Round of 32 knockout — no draws; level after 90 goes to extra time and penalties. Winner advances to the Round of 16.
